sources.name: OpenRouter Models
sources.use_for: Model availability, model IDs, pricing, context length, and provider metadata.
sources.careval_interpretation: Use this as the model catalog, not as a CAREVAL quality score.

sources.name: Artificial Analysis
sources.use_for: General model quality, speed, price, latency, and context comparison.
sources.careval_interpretation: Useful external baseline for model capability and cost, but it does not evaluate invisible care labour.

sources.name: LMArena / Arena
sources.use_for: Crowdsourced pairwise human preference across chat, code, image, video, and agent arenas.
sources.careval_interpretation: Useful signal for user preference, but preference is not the same as care-conscious reasoning.

sources.name: HELM
sources.use_for: Transparent scenario-based academic evaluation across capabilities and safety dimensions.
sources.careval_interpretation: Methodological reference for broad, transparent evaluation design.

sources.name: SWE-bench
sources.careval_interpretation: Useful for coding strength only; not evidence of care awareness.

sources.name: Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard
sources.use_for: Open model reproducible benchmark artifacts and datasets.
sources.careval_interpretation: Useful mainly for open-weight models and reproducibility comparisons.

sources.name: LiveBench
sources.careval_interpretation: Useful as an objective benchmark reference; CAREVAL should remain a separate social-reasoning audit.
